title: 顺水推舟
summary:
Poirot investigates the second-floor room where Colonel Porter was found dead in an armchair with a gunshot wound to his right temple. Chief Constable Greville notes that the death likely occurred a few hours prior, though no one heard the shot. Poirot observes that the room is orderly, but finds it highly unusual that a meticulous man like Porter left no suicide note or letter. The landlady, encountered on the stairs, claims Porter had been depressed and pacing following a coroner's inquest in Wormsley Valley regarding the murder of a friend. She also mentions he had been struggling with money and refusing food.

Later, Poirot meets Francis Claude at the Stagg. Francis confesses that she is related to the man posing as Enoch Yates, identifying him as her distant cousin, Charles. She reveals that her family is in desperate need of money because her husband faces financial ruin and potential imprisonment. Francis admits she conceived a plan to blackmail David Hunter by exploiting a rumor that Rosaline Claude's first husband might still be alive, which would invalidate her inheritance. 

Francis explains that she recruited Charles to impersonate "Robert Andrae" to facilitate the scheme. While the plan initially worked and Hunter agreed to deliver money, the situation turned violent. Francis states that Charles was murdered by the "dangerous" David Hunter. Poirot concludes by questioning if Francis then manipulated Colonel Porter into identifying Charles as "Robert Andrae" to further her objectives.
